summary: Completed testing of 'Join Existing Team' feature for individual registration. Found and fixed a critical backend bug (TypeError: multiple values for team_id). All 13 backend tests pass, all frontend features working correctly.

fixed_during_testing.issue: TypeError: server.Player() got multiple values for keyword argument 'team_id' - caused by IndividualRegistration.model_dump() including team_id which conflicted with explicit team_id parameter

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/teams/available returns list with required fields (team_id, team_number, captain_name, spots_remaining)
Backend: GET /api/teams/available only returns teams with positive spots_remaining
Backend: GET /api/teams/available does NOT return full teams (4 players)
Backend: GET /api/teams/available only returns teams with captains (non-empty teams)
Backend: POST /api/register/individual without team_id still auto-assigns (existing behavior preserved)
Backend: POST /api/register/individual with valid team_id joins that specific team
Backend: POST /api/register/individual with invalid team_id returns 400
Backend: POST /api/register/individual with full team_id returns 400 with 'full' message
Backend: Full team correctly excluded from /api/teams/available
Backend: End-to-end flow - create team, verify in available, join team, verify spots decreased
Frontend: 'I have a team already' checkbox visible when teams are available
Frontend: Checking checkbox reveals captain dropdown with team number, captain name, spots remaining
Frontend: Unchecking checkbox hides dropdown and clears selection
Frontend: Submitting with checkbox checked but no captain selected shows validation error toast
Frontend: Full end-to-end flow - register individual joining Team 8, confirmation page shows correct team number
seed_data_creation: Test players created during registration tests (prefixed with TEST_JOIN, TEST_AUTOASSIGN, TEST_E2E, etc.)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Join existing team feature fully working. The /api/teams/available endpoint returns teams with open spots including team_id, team_number, captain_name, and spots_remaining. Individual registration accepts optional team_id to join a specific team. Frontend shows checkbox 'I have a team already' when teams are available, revealing captain dropdown when checked. Validation prevents submission without captain selection. Bug was fixed in server.py line 426 - model_dump now excludes team_id to prevent duplicate keyword argument error.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: IndividualRegistration model inherits from PlayerCreate and adds team_id field. When creating Player object, code did Player(**player_data.model_dump(), team_id=team['id']) which passed team_id twice - once from model_dump() and once explicitly
reproduction_steps: 1. Call POST /api/register/individual with any valid player data
2. Backend throws TypeError: server.Player() got multiple values for keyword argument 'team_id'
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Changed line 426 in server.py from player_data.model_dump() to player_data.model_dump(exclude={'team_id'}) to exclude the team_id field before passing to Player constructor
